title: Intricately Roasted Moroccan Sea Bass Sellou
description: A cozy Moroccan dish featuring roasted sea bass, this recipe is low in sodium and full of flavor, served in a cataplana vessel in the traditional sellou form. The combination of spices and herbs creates a delicious and aromatic experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the sea bass fillets and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together olive oil, lemon juice, parsley, cilantro, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt-free seasoning blend.
Place the sea bass fillets in a cataplana vessel and brush the mixture evenly over both sides of the fish.
Cover the cataplana and roast the sea bass in the preheated oven for 12 minutes.
Remove the cover and continue roasting for an additional 8 minutes, or until the fish is flaky and cooked through.
Remove the sea bass from the oven and let it rest for 2 minutes before serving.
Serve the sea bass hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
